brand.name: Powerful Wellness
brand.tagline: Feel vibrant again — at every age

offer.what: Health coaching for women 35+ by Dr. Melissa Lockhart, NP. Three tiers: (1) free 15-min discovery call — no-pressure entry point; (2) 1-on-1 personalized ongoing coaching — individualized plan, direct messaging between sessions, NP-informed (labeled 'MOST POPULAR'); (3) 6-Week Coaching Program — structured reset across 5 Pillars of Health: Medications/Medical (GLP-1, LIPOLEAN, Peptides), Fitness, Nutrition, Hormonal Balance, and Community Support. Wellness store also sells ebooks and digital products.
offer.coreOutcome: Lose weight, reclaim energy, and balance hormones — through a clinically grounded, personalized plan built for how a woman's body actually works after 35.
offer.pricePoint: Unknown/custom — no public pricing. Discovery call is free. Coaching packages require a consultation; comparable NP-led programs typically $500–$3,000+ per package.
offer.commitment: Short-term (6-week program) or ongoing (1-on-1 recurring coaching); self-serve for digital products
leadMagnets: Free checklist — 'important information before starting any health/fitness/nutrition program'
Free wellness & health email newsletter / content access

audience.icp: Women 35–55 experiencing midlife energy drain, hormonal shifts (perimenopause/menopause), unexplained weight gain, or poor sleep — who are skeptical of generic wellness advice and want clinical credibility behind their plan.
painPoints: Perimenopause/menopause symptoms with no clear guidance
Tried diets and gym — didn't stick or work at this life stage
Spent decades caring for others; health deprioritized
Skepticism that another wellness program will actually work
segments: Medical weight loss seekers — interested in GLP-1/peptides track (higher urgency, higher ticket)
Lifestyle-only coaching — fitness + nutrition pillar, no Rx
Actively peri/menopausal women — hormonal education resonates most
Content consumers — blog/newsletter readers not yet ready to book

salesCycle.length: 1–4 weeks — free content/checklist → email nurture → discovery call booking → enrollment
salesCycle.trial: Yes — free 15-minute discovery call; the primary conversion event the entire email funnel drives toward
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Discovery call with Dr. Lockhart (human-closed); email's job is to get the call booked and ensure the prospect shows up pre-sold
salesCycle.topObjection: "Is this just another wellness program? Will it actually work for someone my age/situation?" — counter with NP credibility, clinical approach, personalization, and peer social proof

ahaMoment: Completing the free 15-min discovery call and leaving with one actionable step + a clear picture of what coaching looks like. For new clients: receiving their personalized plan after the first coaching session — the moment it feels built for them, not generic.

retention.recurring: 1-on-1 ongoing coaching (repeat/monthly bookings); newsletter for passive audience retention
retention.expansion: Checklist/newsletter subscriber → digital product → 6-week program → 1-on-1 ongoing coaching (ascending value ladder); medical track upsell (GLP-1, peptides consultations)
retention.churnRisk: Coaching dropout around weeks 3–4 of the 6-week program; 6-week grads not transitioned into ongoing 1-on-1; newsletter subscribers who never book a discovery call

lifecycleGaps: No branded welcome/onboarding sequence for new email subscribers (basic ESP auto-welcome at most)
No structured nurture sequence from checklist download → discovery call booking
No pre-call warm-up or post-call follow-up sequence around the discovery call
No in-program re-engagement emails to prevent coaching dropout at weeks 3–4
No post-6-week program retention or upsell to ongoing 1-on-1 coaching
No win-back campaign for inactive subscribers or past clients
No referral/advocacy campaign for satisfied clients
toneVoice: Warm, direct, and empowering — woman-to-woman. Medically credible but never clinical/cold. Permission-based ('when you're ready') not pushy. Key phrases to echo: 'years caring for others / now care for YOU,' 'better version of yourself,' 'vibrant aging.' Avoids shame-based language and fad-forward hype.
compliance: Healthcare-adjacent: Dr. Lockhart is an NP; site displays a Medical Earnings Disclaimer. Offer includes clinical interventions (GLP-1, peptides, LIPOLEAN) — emails must use outcomes framing, not diagnostic claims. Include health disclaimer in footers for the medical track. Review against FTC health-claim guidelines and state NP scope-of-practice rules. Standard CAN-SPAM compliance required.
